title: Netanyahu: Arab leaders don’t care about the Palestinian issue

transcript.text: They don't care about the Palestinian
issue. They care about its effect on the
street,
>> the West Bank, where there is growing
settler violence. Your many members of
your government speak loudly in favor of
annexation. This is an area of dispute
or at least disagreement between you and
President Trump very clearly and it's a
problem for Arab countries with whom you
would putitively want to do deals. Can
you say clearly that, you know, I just
don't understand why you don't say
clearly you will not pursue annexation.
>> Well, I've said clearly something else.
I've said that whatever the solution
will be between us and our Palestinian
neighbors, it will be one in which we
will maintain the sovereign power of
security because the area is too small.
I mean, this is greater London. The
whole thing between Israel, the Judea,
Samaria, the West Bank, and up to the
Jordan River is greater London. So
you're not going to have a regime like
the PA, the Palestinian Authority that
uh teaches its children to be suicide
bombers, that pays for slave, that is
the more terrorists kill Jews, the more
they get paid uh and their families are
taken care of. That's not how you
educate for peace. And until we see a
change there, Israel will have to be the
country that controls security west of
the Jordan. Now, that people say that
that's not full sovereignty. And I said,
"You're right." And that's probably a
>> It's not annexation either. You don't
need to have annexation in order to do
that.
>> Under the Trump plan originally, we were
supposed to annex the main populated
areas by by Israelis. It was a strip
quite significant and leave the rest
because that's not going to be returned
under any any plan. Okay. And then you
said, "Okay, there's this outstanding
question, assuming there'll be genuine
reforms in the Palestinian Authority or
in the Palestinian uh policy there that
recognizes Israel's right to exist,
cease to conduct lawfare and
delegitimization and attempts to drive
us to indefensible lines. When that
changes, you'll have a real discussion."
>> Isn't it important though for your for
your Arab partners as well in order to
say
>> Yes. It's important in public
conversations,
in private conversations. You want the
truth. I mean, beyond beyond the regular
things, many of them don't give a hoot.
>> They didn't care about annexation.
>> They don't care about the Palestinian
issue. They care about its effect on the
street. They themselves say we
understand that there's so much there's
so much uh uh chicainery there and so
much corruption there and that they say
uh you know we need something in order
to have to be to continue the Abraham
Accords which I uh made with President
Trump and the leaders of four Arab
countries and we got four peace treaties
and in fact it's because they put aside
this question and didn't try to drive it
into a wall that we had the peace
agreements with the the Emirates with
Bahrain, with Morocco and Sudan.
